message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>We compared vaccine effectiveness (VE) against severe COVID-19 during calendar periods from December 2021 to March 2022 when Omicron BA.1 and BA.2, respectively, were the dominating virus variants in Scania county, Sweden. We used continuous density case-control sampling matched for sex and age, and with further adjustment for differences in comorbidities and prior infection. VE remained relatively stable after the transition from BA.1 to BA.2 among people with at least three doses but decreased markedly among those with only two doses. Protection from prior infection was also lower after the transition to BA.2. These findings suggest that booster vaccination is needed to maintain sufficient protection against severe COVID-19.</jats:p>
title: COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness against severe disease from the Omicron BA.1 and BA.2 subvariants – surveillance results from southern Sweden, December 2021 to March 2022
